title: Timothée Chalamet Wins Golden Globe for Best Actor Musical or Comedy - Variety
text: Timothée Chalamet claimed his first Golden Globe award on January 11, 2026, winning Best Actor in a Musical or Comedy for his role in Marty Supreme, directed by Josh Safdie. The actor defeated notable competitors including George Clooney, Leonardo DiCaprio, Ethan Hawke, Lee Byung-hun, and Jesse Plemons. His win came after receiving widespread critical acclaim and winning precursor awards, including the Critics Choice Award. During his remarks, Chalamet reflected on his previous nominations without victories, stating: Those moments didn't make this moment that much sweeter. He credited his father's influence on maintaining gratitude, expressing appreciation for director Safdie and his co-stars including Gwyneth Paltrow, Fran Drescher, and Kevin O'Leary. Marty Supreme follows a fictional ping-pong champion pursuing his dream, with the film earning $84 million globally.
